revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:福良天満宮]]
{{infobox religious building | name = Fukura Tenmangū|native_name = Fukura Tenmangū (福良天満宮) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|image =File:Fukuratenmanguusuki.JPG | address = {{ill|Usuki, Ōita|ja|臼村市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usuki, Ōita|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Fukurachō 211 | 緯度度 = 33| 緯度分 = 06 | 緯度秒 = 56 | 経度度 = 131| 経度分 = 47 | 経度秒 = 41 | 地図 = Japan Oita | 祭神 = {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|菊原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q382005}} | 社格 = | 創建 = Around 1590 | 本殿 = | 別名 = Fukura-no-miya, Tenman Shrine (former name) | 札所等 = | 例祭 = Late July | 神事 = | location = {{ill|Usuki|qid=Q845792|1=Usuki, Ōita}} | coordinates = {{Coord|33.115556|N|131.794722|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }}
'''Fukura Tenmangū''' (福良天満宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} ({{ill|Tenmangū|en|Tenmangū|qid=Q798838|lt=Tenmangū|1=Tenmangū}}) located in {{ill|Usuki, Ōita|ja|臼村市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usuki, Ōita|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Japan. It enshrines {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|菊原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q382005}}, the deity commonly known as "Tenjin-sama."
== History == After {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|菊原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q382005}} was exiled to {{ill|Dazaifu (government)|en|Dazaifu (government)|qid=Q144845|lt=Dazaifu, Fukuoka|1=Dazaifu (government)}} and passed away in 903 ({{ill|Engi (era)|en|Engi (era)|qid=Q1374325|lt=Engi (era)|1=Engi (era)}} 3), his descendants took the surname Ohata and continued to protect his image in {{ill|Suō Province|en|Suō Province|qid=Q1053037|lt=Suō Province|1=Suō Province}}. During the {{ill|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|en|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|qid=Q1145058|lt=Tenshō Era|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} period (circa 1590), they fled from Toyotomi Hideyoshi's national unification campaigns and brought the enshrined deity to {{ill|Kaifu District, Bungo Province|ja|海都郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaifu District, Bungo Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Fukura Manor.<ref name="about">{{Cite web|title=History|publisher=Fukura Tenmangū|url=http://fukuragu.jp/wp/about|accessdate=2018-12-20}}</ref>
In the early {{ill|Keichō|en|Keichō|qid=Q1328185|lt=Keichō|1=Keichō}} era (circa 1600), Monk Enyo established {{ill|Ryūgen-ji (Usuki)|ja|龍原寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryūgen-ji (Usuki)|1=Ryūgen-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q60988552}} at Tairashimizu and Shōmyō-ji in Fukura Village.<ref name="about" />
In 1602 (Keichō 13), {{ill|Inaba Sadamichi|ja|稲葉伟通|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inaba Sadamichi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, lord of Usuki Castle, lost a hawk during a hunt. After praying at a small shrine at the foot of Chinzan Mountain, the hawk returned to a pine tree nearby. In gratitude, he relocated the shrine, first to Miyabata and later to its current location within Shōmyō-ji, naming it "Yotaka-den" (呼鷹殿).<ref name="about" />
Shōmyō-ji later converted to {{ill|Shingon Buddhism|en|Shingon Buddhism|qid=Q550182|lt=Shingon Buddhism|1=Shingon Buddhism}}, received the title Sugiōin from {{ill|Ninna-ji|en|Ninna-ji|qid=Q1202871|lt=Ninna-ji|1=Ninna-ji}}, and was successively renamed Sugashōin Tenman-ji, Bairinzan Tenman-ji, and finally Zuiō-in Tenman-ji under {{ill|Tiantai|en|Tiantai|qid=Q668188|lt=Tendai Buddhism|1=Tiantai}}. The shrine received patronage from the {{ill|Inaba clan|en|Inaba clan|qid=Q2975276|lt=Inaba clan|1=Inaba clan}}, who revered Sugawara no Michizane.<ref name="about" />
In 1868 ({{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治復興|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji Restoration|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), following the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|en|Shinbutsu bunri|qid=Q1970535|lt=Shinbutsu bunri|1=Shinbutsu bunri}}, it was renamed Tenman Shrine. In 1895 (Meiji 28), the current main hall was constructed.<ref name="about" />
During the {{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|qid=Q327567|lt=Shōwa era|1=Shōwa era}}, the shrine was renamed Fukura Tenmangū. In 1982 (Shōwa 57), it was used as a filming location for "{{ill|Otoko wa Tsurai yo: Hana mo Arashi mo Torajirō|ja|男はつらいよ 花も嵐も虎次郎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otoko wa Tsurai yo: Hana mo Arashi mo Torajirō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}."<ref>{{Cite web|title=Torasan filming location|publisher=Fukura Tenmangū|url=http://fukuragu.jp/tora|accessdate=2018-12-20}}</ref> In 2002 (Heisei 14), the 1100th anniversary festival of Sugawara no Michizane was held.
== Festivals == A summer festival is held for three days in late July, featuring {{ill|Kagura|en|Kagura|qid=Q1348260|lt=Kagura|1=Kagura}} performances and Akajishi-ren (red lion dance).<ref>{{Cite web|title=Fukura Tenmangū Summer Festival|publisher=Tourism Oita|url=https://www.visit-oita.jp/events/detail/9595/|accessdate=2018-12-20}}</ref> The third day features a mikoshi procession and a popular "Yakumo-harai" (serpent exorcism) ritual. It is one of Usuki’s three major summer festivals.<ref name="event07">{{Cite web|title=Summer Festival|publisher=Fukura Tenmangū|url=http://fukuragu.jp/event/event07|accessdate=2018-12-20}}</ref>
== Subordinate Shrines == * '''{{nihongo|Yuga Shrine|瑜伽神社}}''' – Enshrines {{ill|Ame-no-Tajikarao|ja|天手力男神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Tajikarao|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アメノタヂカラオ|1=Ame-no-Tajikarao|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q849412}} and {{ill|Shirafuji-no-Tayū|ja|白太夫神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirafuji-no-Tayū|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 渡会春彦|1=Shirafuji-no-Tayū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q92665997}}.<ref name="event07" /> A branch shrine of {{ill|Yuga Shrine (Kurashiki)|ja|田秋山 \u 瑜伽神社本宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuga Shrine (Kurashiki)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Fukura Tenmangū Summer Festival|publisher=Kurashiki Yuga Shrine|url=http://www.yugasan.or.jp/gyoujihoukoku/2011/2011fukura/fukura2011.html|accessdate=2018-12-20}}</ref> * '''Atago Shrine''' – Enshrines {{ill|Ukemochi|en|Ukemochi|qid=Q2280323|lt=Ukemochi|1=Ukemochi}} and {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|ja|火産霊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagu-tsuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カグツチ}}.<ref name="event07" /> * '''Okunomiya Akameko Shrine''' – Built in 1999 (Heisei 11). Hosts the Usuki Akameko Festival in early May.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Usuki Akameko Festival|publisher=Fukura Tenmangū|url=http://fukuragu.jp/event/event05|accessdate=2018-12-20}}</ref>
== References ==
<references />
== External links ==
* [http://fukuragu.com/ Official website]{{Yuga Faith}}
